Campaign Finance Information Center

August 1998
Government & PoliticsData & DatabasesJournalism & Newsroom

The Campaign Finance Information Clearinghouse offers freely downloadable campaign finance data from 13 states and links to 16 additional search engines, with plans to launch a universal search tool allowing users to track contributors across states.

Campaign finance data from 13 states is freely downloadable from the CFIC at www.campaignfinance.org We also have links to sixteen online search engines hosted by other non-profits and state boards of election. We will soon release our "universal" online search engine so you can type in a contributor from your state and see where else they are giving.

The more inclusive this database, the better for everyone. So, if you have state or local campaign data you want included, contact CFIC Coordinator Jack Dolan at jack@nicar.org or (573) 884-1802. The CFIC credits everyone who contributes data.

Other services available on the CFIC Web site include a directory of campaign finance reporters, a collection of campaign finance stories and other suggested readings to generate story ideas, and information on joining the CFIC-L mailing list.
